coin_profile.name: Hyperliquid
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Hyperliquid (HYPE) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #11 by market cap.

HYPE.name: Hyperliquid

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: HYPE lacks a meaningful protocol moat despite ranking #11 by market cap. The critical 'dilution_control' score of 1 indicates severe tokenomics risk with only 22.24% circulating supply, creating massive future selling pressure that undermines token holder value accrual. While it shows decent protocol maturity (8) and network demand (6), absent competitive defensibility against forks, weak ecosystem lock-in, and a cash-flow model dependent on continued fee generation with minimal actual utility capture for token holders (score 5), HYPE resembles a high-ranking but structurally fragile protocol vulnerable to competitor displacement.

chart_zones.mos_note: Cycle phase (Distribution (near ATH)) not favourable for entry — wait for accumulation or early uptrend.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.8/10 and survivability 46.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.8/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 46.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Metcalfe Network Value is shown in the breakdown chart without contributing to the median: the implied IV is below 10% of current price, indicating the token is trading at a significant premium to its network activity level. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — future token unlocks are expected to create selling pressure. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

HYPE.iv_method: Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, Dilution Adjustment

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

metric_assessments.holder_value_accrual: Holder Value Accrual is middling. The numbers are acceptable but leave room for improvement.

metric_assessments.dilution_control: Dilution Control is a concern (22.24 circulating %). This dimension is below par and worth monitoring closely.

Its clearest strength is protocol maturity, while dilution control remains the main area to watch. Key risks: future token dilution (FDV/MC ratio: 4.5x); low dilution control score (1/10); only 22% of supply currently circulating. Hyperliquid warrants caution and should only be held as a small position with a clear thesis and defined risk limits.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: Hyperliquid scores 4.8/10 (Dangerous) as a Cash-Flow Protocol. The overall quality profile reflects the protocol's maturity, fee economics, and token supply dynamics relative to peers.

coin_profile.name: Hedera
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Hedera is an enterprise-grade public distributed ledger using hashgraph consensus technology rather than a traditional blockchain. It offers fast, fair, and secure transactions at low cost, and is governed by a council of major corporations including Google, IBM, and Deutsche Telekom.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: HBAR has moderate defensibility through its proprietary hashgraph consensus and enterprise partnerships (IBM, Google), but lacks network effects comparable to settlement layers—growth in users doesn't meaningfully increase value capture for token holders. Token utility is weak (dilution control 8/10 but holder_value_accrual 5/10), with most value accrual flowing to enterprise validators rather than HBAR stakers, and ecosystem lock-in is limited as the network competes directly with cheaper L1s (Solana, Polygon) on throughput alone. Regulatory resilience is moderate; while decentralized governance is improving, the network's historical reliance on Hedera-controlled validators and enterprise backing creates centralization risks that undermine moat strength relative to more proven settlement layers.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

coin_profile.name: HTX DAO
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: HTX DAO (HTX) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #48 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: HTX lacks meaningful protocol moat due to weak competitive defensibility (easily forked exchange model with no novel tech), minimal network effects (users optimize for lowest fees/best execution rather than switching costs), and limited value capture (token primarily governs fee discounts rather than capturing sustainable cash flows). While dilution control scores reasonably (8/10), this is insufficient to offset fundamental vulnerabilities: centralized exchange tokenomics are commoditized, regulatory arbitrage provides temporary advantage but not structural defensibility, and ecosystem lock-in is negligible as users and liquidity migrate across venues based on incentive structures rather than switching costs.

coin_profile.name: Helium
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Helium (HNT) — a Cash-Flow Protocol ranked #231 by market cap.

dilution_control.label: Dilution Control
dilution_control.chart_ref: Network Economics → Token Supply: Issuance vs Burns

moat.rationale: HNT's moat is weak despite its first-mover advantage in decentralized wireless infrastructure. The protocol suffers from low protocol maturity (score: 1), poor holder value accrual (score: 4), and modest network demand (score: 4), indicating that token holders capture minimal economic value from network growth. While the physical infrastructure of Helium hotspots creates some switching costs, the protocol has demonstrated vulnerability to competitive forks (Solana's migration, emergence of alternatives like Helium Mobile), and the underlying wireless use case lacks the irreplaceable settlement properties of Layer 1s or the network density effects of established social networks.

chart_zones.mos_note: Price above speculative fair value — no margin of safety.

2026.net_issuance_note: Current snapshot only. Historical supply data requires on-chain indexing not available via free APIs.

meta.data_note: No protocol fee or TVL data found for this token on DeFiLlama. Charts 1 and 2 are unavailable. Charts 3 and 4 show current-year supply and dilution data from CoinGecko.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 4.0/10 and survivability 36.7%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (4.0/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 36.7% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 2 methods contributed to the median. More data sources (DeFiLlama fee history) would improve confidence in this estimate. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or heavily overextended price relative to value — higher-quality opportunities available elsewhere.

bull_points: <cite index="1-12">Helium is testing key technical resistance (as of May 2026), with a sharp rally facing a crucial hurdle that could determine if the uptrend continues</cite>, offering a near-term breakout opportunity.
<cite index="1-13">Grayscale's inclusion of HNT in its Q2 2026 watchlist signals institutional research interest</cite>, which could catalyze institutional capital inflows over the next 3-6 months.

<cite index="1-14">Helium's migration to Solana and expanding mobile subscriber base are cited as key sector drivers</cite>, with the network now supporting real utility that should attract positive ecosystem coverage in the near term.
bear_points: <cite index="1-6">Strong network fundamentals have failed to translate into meaningful price recovery, creating investor fatigue and skepticism about the token's value accrual</cite>, which risks prolonged consolidation.

<cite index="4-28,4-29">Helium is showing clear bearish momentum against USDT, with a 'Strong Sell' technical rating reflecting both technical indicators and moving averages pointing to downside pressure</cite>.

<cite index="5-4,5-5,5-6">HNT faces immediate resistance between $1.75-$2.00, and failure to establish daily closes above $2.00 would likely keep the asset oscillating between $1.30 and $1.90</cite>, limiting near-term upside.

bull_points: <cite index="9-1">The August 2025 halving cut annual HNT issuance from 15M to 7.5M, while a policy to burn 100% of Helium Mobile subscriber revenue aims for net deflation</cite>, creating favorable long-term supply dynamics for value accrual.

<cite index="9-12,9-13">Helium's network supports over 366,000 hotspots, has offloaded over 2,700 TB of mobile data for major carriers, and daily active users have surpassed 1 million</cite>, demonstrating real-world utility that should compound adoption.

<cite index="9-15,9-16">Every data transaction burns HNT via Data Credits, creating inherent, usage-driven demand—as the network expands and carrier adoption accelerates, the velocity and scale of these burns could become the primary price driver</cite> over a 1-5 year horizon.
bear_points: The provided network economics data shows no historical annual fee or PS ratio data for Helium, leaving the protocol's long-term economic durability and fee sustainability unproven across multiple market cycles.

<cite index="1-21">Despite rising usage metrics, the token has failed to deliver meaningful price recovery</cite>, raising fundamental questions about whether Data Credit burns will ever outpace post-halving emission schedules and translate usage into value accrual.

As a DePIN protocol operating in wireless infrastructure, Helium faces long-term disruption risk from established telecom incumbents, regulatory changes to spectrum allocation, or competing decentralized network protocols that could erode its competitive moat over 3-5 years.

bull_points: <cite index="1-25,1-26">Holo network opened to developers in July 2025 with HummHive as the first live application, signaling real utility deployment</cite> that could drive adoption momentum and positive sentiment over the next 3-6 months.

<cite index="3-2,3-3">HoloworldAI platform integration and Binance listing feature a revenue-driven buyback mechanism that creates deflationary pressure and aligns token holder interests</cite>, providing a structural catalyst for near-term price support.

<cite index="3-4,3-11">Wind Tunnel Stress Testing (March 2026) represents a bullish protocol upgrade that tests network robustness under real-world load conditions, increasing reliability for hosting applications</cite> and potentially attracting institutional adoption interest.
bear_points: <cite index="3-1,3-12">Recent market sentiment shows HOT declining 11.48% with negative short-term performance within the competitive DePIN sector</cite>, indicating weak momentum despite network developments.

<cite index="10-2,10-5,10-6">Technical analysis reflects a bearish market outlook supported by extreme fear sentiment (Fear & Greed Index of 20), with only 40% green days in the past 30 days indicating sustained selling pressure</cite>.

<cite index="4-30,4-31">Analyst consensus projects 2026 as a slow but important development year requiring real usage adoption of Holochain applications rather than sharp price rallies</cite>, limiting near-term upside expectations absent major catalysts.

bull_points: <cite index="1-4,1-5">The HOT token serves as a placeholder for HoloFuel, designed as a stable currency backed by computing power with innovative regulation mechanisms to achieve stability superior to other cryptocurrencies</cite>, creating long-term value for protocol participants.

<cite index="7-19">100% of circulating supply is already in circulation with FDV matching market cap, eliminating future dilution risk for long-term token holders</cite> and supporting sustainable value accrual over a 1-5 year horizon.

<cite index="4-3,4-36">Holo demonstrates long-term potential through low-cost, high-performance DApp hosting for developers, with growth depending on sustained developer adoption and real-world usage of decentralized applications</cite> as the infrastructure backbone for Web3 adoption.
bear_points: The network economics data shows no multi-year fee data exists for Holo—the protocol's long-term economic durability and sustainable fee generation remain unproven, creating uncertainty about whether network value will accrue to token holders over a 3-5 year horizon.

<cite index="4-20,4-31">Long-term growth depends critically on whether developers and small-scale platforms actually adopt Holochain for lightweight applications, representing significant execution risk if adoption fails to materialize as expected</cite>.

Technology disruption risk from competing decentralized infrastructure solutions (DePIN protocols, layer-2 scaling, or alternative peer-to-peer networks) cannot be ruled out over a 1-5 year horizon, potentially making HOT's competitive position unsustainable.
